w:r.w:t: Biogenix Client Requirement Difference Table
Source reviewed: Biogenix Discussion.ods, sheet Ques & Ans

Overall verdict: The current system is partially aligned. The foundation for role and company pricing exists, but bulk pricing, coupon logic, quantity-rule split, and full priority handling are not implemented yet.
Row
Client Requirement
Current Support
Status
Minimum Backend Change
Bulk discount configurable for all users, specific users, or roles
Only preview UI exists. No backend bulk pricing model was found.
Not aligned
Add scoped bulk pricing records and resolve them in the backend pricing service.
Bulk discount with product discount should follow priority
Current backend applies only the selected price row plus that row's own discount.
Not aligned
Extend pricing logic to apply bulk by rule priority.
Guest, B2C, B2B, and Company pricing with company first
Company price already gets priority, but current price buckets do not match the exact four client terms.
Partially aligned
Map or simplify price buckets to the client pricing model.
Different min and max quantity for B2C and B2B
Min, max, and lot size come only from the variant, so all users receive the same rule.
Not aligned
Add user-type-specific quantity rule storage and runtime resolution.
Priority should be Contract > Coupon > Bulk > Product Discount > Base
Current backend supports company price first and one product discount only.
Not aligned
Build one final-price resolver that accepts user, quantity, and optional coupon.
Coupon applicability should depend on contract, bulk, and existing discount
Coupon handling is frontend-only on checkout and is not part of backend pricing.
Not aligned
Add coupon master tables and backend applicability rules.
Bulk pricing should replace base price, not act as extra discount
No backend bulk pricing engine exists today.
Not aligned
Store bulk as quantity-based price rows instead of view-only display rules.
Use fixed priority instead of auto-selecting the lowest price
Role and company pricing already follow a fixed order, but not the full client hierarchy.
Partially aligned
Extend fixed-priority logic to coupon, bulk, discount, and base layers.
Min and max quantity should be configurable per product and per user type
Quantity rules are variant-only and not user-type-specific.
Not aligned
Add quantity-rule records by product or variant and by user type.
